AR1.justification: The new entry was routed to today's daybook (the chronological catch-all) with a Guardrails note rather than mutating decisions.md or inventing an observation, matching the methodology's strict bars for each register.

AR2.justification: The agent read decisions.md, observations.md, the latest daybook, and daybook/AGENTS.md before creating daybook/2026-05-17.md, so each procedural step produced or consumed a concrete on-disk artifact.

AR3.justification: Cross-references use relative paths (decisions.md, daybook/2026-04-22.md, daybook/2026-05-17.md) with an ISO date anchor (2026-04-15) that resolves cleanly to the accepted decision entry.

AR4.justification: Actions are append-only: a new dated daybook file is created and decisions.md is read-only, so re-running would either no-op on the same date or append a new day's entry without corrupting history.
